title: Greg James Rides a Bike to Help People
summary: Greg James, a radio host, rode a special bike for 1,000 kilometers to raise money for Comic Relief, a charity that helps people in need. He started in Weymouth and finished in Edinburgh, riding through England, Wales, and Scotland. Along the way, famous friends like Joe Lycett and Prince William joined him on the bike. People cheered for him and sent messages of support. Greg worked hard and felt emotional, but he was happy to help others. In the end, he raised over £4 million, which is a lot of money for charity. This was his third big challenge for Comic Relief, and he did it to honor his dad, who had a stroke. His parents are very proud of him.

background_read: Comic Relief is a charity that raises money to help people in the UK and around the world. Greg James is a popular radio host on BBC Radio 1. He likes to do big challenges to support good causes. In this challenge, he rode a tandem bike, which has two seats, so friends could join him. Riding a bike for a long time is hard work, but it can make a big difference for people who need help.
Article_Structure: WHO: Greg James, a radio host, and his famous friends like Joe Lycett and Prince William
WHAT: He rode a tandem bike for 1,000 kilometers to raise money for Comic Relief
WHERE: He rode from Weymouth to Edinburgh, through England, Wales, and Scotland
WHY: To help people in need and honor his dad, who had a stroke

perspectives.description: Greg James worked hard to raise money for a good cause, showing how people can help others.
